preview_text:
The model favors Germany at 61%, with Ivory Coast given a 12% chance of victory. This represents a substantial gap: Germany enters as a clear favorite, though the implied margin between the two leaves room for upset probability that reflects genuine match uncertainty at this stage of the tournament.

The draw probability of 27% sits at the high end of typical knockout-round spreads, suggesting a competitive fixture where a stalemate remains plausible. The win-probability gap between the teams narrows considerably when draws are factored in—both sides carry meaningful chances to avoid defeat. This points toward a match where defensive solidity and set-piece execution may prove decisive.

Model and sportsbook are closely aligned on the Germany win probability, differing by only 0.2 percentage points. The market assigns draw odds about 4 points lower than the model, while correspondingly pricing Ivory Coast roughly 4 points higher. These gaps are modest enough that both sources suggest a similar competitive landscape, though the market leans slightly toward a decisive result and a stronger Ivory Coast case than the model's estimate.
